title: Learning Effectiveness, Dependency Viz & Revert Mode
requirements:
Measure standing instruction impact. Visualize ticket dependencies. Mechanical undo for bad merges.

1. Learning effectiveness scoring:
   - Update agent-learning.schema.json: add runs_with_instruction (integer), effectiveness_score (number 0-1)
   - Update scripts/agent-learning.sh: increment-runs increments runs_with_instruction for active entries, lifecycle computes effectiveness_score on every transition, new action 'prune' auto-dismisses entries with effectiveness < 0.5 after 10+ runs (mark as 'ineffective')
   - Add 'ineffective' to status enum in schema

2. Dependency graph script:
   - Create scripts/dependency-graph.sh: Args: <ticket_id>
   - Walks Jira linked issues (blocked-by, blocks) up to depth 3
   - For each linked ticket: fetch status + PR status
   - Output modes: --json (structured JSON nodes + edges), --ascii (ASCII art tree), --dot (DOT format for Graphviz)
   - Color coding: green=done, yellow=in-progress, red=blocked, grey=not-started

3. Dashboard dependencies tab:
   - Add Dependencies tab per ticket
   - Render dependency graph (simple SVG or text-based)
   - Show status of each linked ticket, highlight blocking path

4. Revert mode:
   - Add --revert flag to parse-args.sh (usage: PROJ-123 --revert), sets mode: 'revert'
   - Create scripts/revert-run.sh: Args: <ticket_id>
     - Look up archived artifacts in runs/{ticket_id}/
     - Find merged PR number + merge commit from PRD.json
     - Create revert branch: {code}_{ticket_id}_revert_{type}
     - Create worktree at standard path
     - Run git revert <merge_commit> --no-edit in worktree
     - Run tests via toolchain (resolve-toolchain.sh test) -- fail = escalate
     - Run secret scan -- fail = escalate
     - Skip Critic review entirely (mechanical undo)
     - Open PR linking to original PR with revert context
     - If jira: post comment on ticket noting revert
   - Output: revert PR URL

5. Add revert to Makefile:
   - Add revert target: claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions '/start $(TICKET) --revert'

6. Write tests:
   - Test effectiveness scoring after 10 runs with no recurrence
   - Test prune action on low-effectiveness entry
   - Test dependency-graph.sh output formats
   - Test revert mode with a previously merged ticket
   - Test revert mode with test failure (expect escalation)
acceptance_criteria: agent-learning.schema.json has runs_with_instruction and effectiveness_score fields
agent-learning.sh increment-runs updates runs_with_instruction for active entries
agent-learning.sh prune marks entries with effectiveness < 0.5 after 10+ runs as ineffective
Dashboard shows Dependencies tab per ticket with graph visualization
parse-args.sh supports --revert flag setting mode to revert
scripts/revert-run.sh creates revert branch, runs tests and secret scan, opens PR
